video_title: Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery | Date Announcement | Netflix
comment_text: I’m breathless with excitement for this! After Poirot (the *REAL* Hercule Poirot aka Sir David Suchet, not the farcically mustachioed buffoon in the recent, splashy Hollywood trash adaptations of Poirot mysteries), Benoit Blanc is easily my favorite fictional detective. Brilliant, charming, kindly, and pitted against the most devilishly tricky and clever whodunnits this side of Agatha Christie, I’m thrilled to have a proper successor to Poirot in our modern day in Benoit!

video_title: Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery | Date Announcement | Netflix
comment_text:
This movie is inspired by John Dickson Carr, "the king of the art of misdirection" according to Agatha Christie. For me, Carr is the best mystery writer ever.

Carr is also the king of the locked room mystery and mixed gothic, horror and detection. I expect at least a locked room murder case in this movie.

Rian Johnson was reading Carr's novels since 2023 and was invited to write a text about him (it was published as a preface on "The Problem of the Wire Cage").
